entry.title: S/4 vs. R/3: Changes in reporting for controlling
entry.content:
Scope of the note

This note addresses the classic Report Painter and Report Writer reports in S/4HANA, with a particular focus on Cost Center Accounting and Internal Orders reports, which display actual costs on the subsequent page. Reports outlined with a red border include additional features detailed in the chapter "Special Posting Information and Its Effects." Furthermore, this note encompasses the standard FIORI Analytical Apps available in the system, based on Web Dynpro technology, specifically "Cost Centers Plan/Actual" and "Internal Orders Plan/Actual," along with their underlying data structures. Customer-specific apps or views are not covered in this note.

Figure 1: FIORI W0081: Technical Info: Cost Center Plan/Actual

Figure 2: FIORI W0076: Technical Info: Internal Order Plan/Actual

Figure 3: Painter Reports: Cost Center Accounting

Figure 4: Painter Reports: Internal Orders

 

Technical description of the evolution of the value model

The value model in the SAP accounting system has changed significantly over recent years and decades. Before the introduction of the New General Ledger (NewGL), a dual-ledger system was in place, rooted in the classic Central European approach of separating controlling and financial accounting. This setup meant that discrepancies between the two areas were common, at least throughout the year, and had to be reconciled during the period-end closing process. As the number and complexity of transactions increased, so did the effort needed to align the two ledgers. In this system, the classic general ledger primarily served financial accounting, while profit center accounting was used for the controlling area.

Figure 5: Changes in Accounting

As part of the introduction of the New General Ledger, the special ledgers and, in particular, profit center accounting were integrated with financial accounting. Depending on the configuration, the profit center was now posted with almost all documents, making it more a part of financial accounting than controlling. With the introduction of the new S/4HANA enterprise software generation, the unification of these two areas was further developed and expanded. A Universal Journal was created, now encompassing asset and material accounting in addition to financial accounting and controlling, resulting in a single-ledger system. This shift was reflected in the data structure, including the table landscape.

This understanding is crucial when navigating between these two worlds and using applications from the respective systems. To illustrate this better, the above diagram (Figure 5) is enhanced with report technologies. See the diagram on the next page.

 

Change in reporting

Figure 6: Development of Reporting

At first glance, the significantly enhanced user experience is striking. The new apps are much more modern and up-to-date. The approach to reporting has also changed fundamentally. Classic reports are application-specific, resulting in numerous reports for various purposes. In contrast, the modern FIORI Analytical Apps emphasize flexibility and function similarly to a pivot table in terms of features and usability. For example, in the 'Plan/Actual Cost Centers' app, you can customize the report for various purposes by selecting columns and rows, saving it as individual views, and making it accessible to other users.

In addition to the user experience and functionality, the way data is visualized has also fundamentally changed. The following diagram illustrates this:

Figure 7: Perspectives on Data

In the past, each area or stakeholder had its own data stored in separate tables tailored to their specific needs. This often led to differences in displays and values, particularly between controlling and financial accounting. While a financial accountant only saw the actual posted values, the controller was also interested in statistical and imputed values.

The following rule of thumb can thus be internalized:

While in R/3, users with different perspectives also looked at different data, in S/4 users with different perspectives look at the same data basis.

For a more in-depth technical explanation, see the following note:
2185026 - Compatibility views COSP, COSS, COEP, COVP: How do you optimize their use? - SAP for Me

And blog entry:

Analytics on Universal Journal, the heart of SAP S/4HANA | SAP Blogs

 

Special posting information and it’s impact

While the strategic approach in S/4HANA is to consolidate data into a few tables, relevant non-financial accounting data will still be stored in the classic CO tables, as outlined in the following chapter.

The use of classic Painter reports also follows the above mentioned dual-ledger system in S/4HANA. The following sections introduce special types of information and their effects on both classic and new reporting.

 

Splitting

Cost splitting information, such as the breakdown into fixed and variable portions, is stored in both the classic CO tables (COSP_BAK and COSS_BAK) and the Universal Journal (ACDOCA). To display all information, it’s necessary to combine data from both sources. As of the date of this document (02/12/2024), a custom CDS view should be considered for a complete and easy display of splitting information.

 

Statistical Postings

Statistical postings are entries made on statistical elements, such as internal orders or Project System (PSP) elements. Within these elements, a 'real' cost center to be charged is recorded. Thus, when posting to these statistical elements, a statistical entry is made on the statistical element, and a real entry is made on the recorded cost center. The same applies to secondary allocations. In the new FIORI analytical apps, a specific feature is available for this purpose, which can be selected or deselected. Refer to the following figure for more details.

Figure 8: Statistical Characteristics

The information is largely contained within the central universal ledger, the ACDOCA table. However, an exception is the information on 'Cost Center/Order/WBS Element is statistical,' which is not included in ACDOCA but instead determined in CDS views. Additionally, these fields are not available in all apps (e.g., Journal Entry Analyzer, Display Line Items in the General Ledger) or as filters. To display or hide statistical postings, selection criteria should be reviewed carefully, and corresponding views or bookmarks should be centrally configured.

 

Commitment

In SAP, an 'obligation' refers to a commitment typically created by a purchase order on an account assignment object. This information is essential for a complete budget overview. The choice of solution is crucial when analyzing this data. The classic obligation solution continues to store its data in the legacy CO tables. If the new obligation management is activated, this information is retrieved from an extension ledger. If both solutions are active—since not all account assignment objects (currently only cost centers) are supported in the new solution—it is important to evaluate the correct source based on the active solution. For the new solution, the corresponding extension ledger must be selected in the ACDOCA table.

 

Work in Progress (WIP) Postings

Due to the initial separation of the two systems described above, classic CO reports rely on different selection criteria (specifically the object number). When evaluating an account assignment object, only the information where this field is populated is displayed. Work-in-progress (WIP) represents a balance sheet item for goods tied up in the production process.

For a more detailed explanation, please refer to the following entry in SAP Help:                                                                     https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP_SPV/1352f25ee3eb4c888ae33f034441e4f9/ebfe8c53d7d8ec23e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US&version=6.03.23

These financial postings are populated with the 'Order' field and are therefore not included in an order line item report. However, since these postings are part of the Universal Journal, they are generally displayed in the new FIORI Analytical Apps.

In the context of Universal Parallel Accounting or the Public Cloud version with event-based product costing, the behavior is similar. In this setup, as all documents are recorded in the Universal Journal, they can be displayed with account assignment directly at the WIP accrual. However, this is not the case in older reports based on the COSB table. For a deeper understanding of the differences between traditional WIP creation and release and the new approach, I recommend the following blog post:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/the-differences-between-traditional-and-event-based-wip-and-variance/ba-p/13539406

 

Missing or Overly Broad Filters

The expanded flexibility in the new FIORI Apps also requires users to exercise greater attention. The fields 'Cost Center / Order Statistical' and 'Account Assignment Type' are particularly sensitive.

Field „Cost Center / Order Statistical“
When this field is selected and visible in the report, statistical postings are also displayed. If the 'Account Assignment Type' field is also active, it indicates where the 'real' posting has been directed. For example, in a cost center report, it will display:

 

Cost Center Statistical

Account Assignment Type

G/L Account

Actual Amount

X

PR

63007000

5.000

A statistical posting of 5,000 has been made to a cost center, which has been genuinely assigned to a WBS element.

It should also be noted that this display differs from the classic reports. In the classic reports, this information was sometimes available on an additional report page (see Figure 9) or not available at all, as it was not CO-relevant (e.g., GR/IR). See Figure 8.

Figure 9: Report Comparison - Statistical Information

 

Field: „Account Assignment Type“

This field refers to the account assignment object that was primarily posted. The following selections are typically possible:

Field Entry

Description

KS

Cost Center

KL

Activity Type

PR

Project (Work-Breakdown-Structure)

OR

Order

EO

Profitability Segment


This means it may be useful to exclude result-object-relevant postings [EO] in a cost center report and, conversely, to exclude all other values in the 'Market Segments Plan/Actual' report. The effect of an overly broad selection is illustrated in Figure 10 on the next page, where no restrictions were applied other than the controlling area:

Figure 10 : FIORI Analytical App without filter

You can see that, in addition to the postings from margin analysis, values without controlling-relevant assignment, such as pure FI postings, are also displayed. Consequently, the informational value is very low.

If attributed account assignment is used, for example, within the framework of project-oriented manufacturing, both project-related costs and positions relevant to margin analysis are visible in the Universal Journal. For further information on this type of account assignment, please refer to the following blog entry:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/using-market-segments-in-margin-analysis/ba-p/13528676

 

What to Do in Case of Discrepancies?

The following steps should be taken if there are discrepancies between GUI/Painter reports and FIORI Analytical Apps:

  1. Check your selection or any preset views and bookmarks.
  2. Specifically check if statistical information is being displayed.
  3. Ensure that the correct currencies are being compared. The new apps display multiple currency types (controlling area currency, company code currency, etc.). GUI reports are usually in controlling area currency.

Figure 11: Currency Types

  1. Try to keep the selection narrow initially and proceed account by account.
  2. Drill down into the line items or call up a line item report to check which, if any, controlling-relevant account assignments are present in the document.
  3. The FIORI Analytical Apps are based on embedded BW technology. If the discrepancies cannot be explained with the above measures, check whether custom information structures have been created and how they are configured (see also Figures 1 and 2).
  4. If you are using custom Painter reports, review those as well.
  5. Painter/Writer reports are consistent in the classic ERP reporting world and function smoothly. The same applies to the new FIORI Analytical Apps in the S/4HANA reporting environment (see also Figure 7).

Breaks in user experience can typically be avoided if the recommendations in the next section are followed.

General Recommendations for Consulting

Wherever possible, focus on the new reporting options. When using FIORI Apps, predefined views and bookmarks for key evaluation purposes should be provided by a knowledgeable central unit (Controlling or IT). If classic reports remain indispensable, as is the case with standard cost, splitting, and internal sales reports, a short training session should be conducted. In general, a department-specific training session is recommended when introducing S/4HANA to better understand the new features.

To meet the needs of different reporting stakeholders and consolidate various reports and their sources, it is advisable to establish a company-wide unified reporting concept and centrally coordinate it.

entry.title: Where can I learn how to implement Document Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing Document Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey.


Overview
In this Learning Journey consultants will learn how to implement Document Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Product Lifecycle Management.

Learning objectives
After completing this Learning Journey, consultants will be able to 

  • Work with documents
  • Store files in secure storage areas
  • Do relevant customizing

Prerequisites

  • Applying SAP S/4HANA Product Engineering

Practice your skills in a live SAP environment

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: I am a consultant. Where can I start learning how the basic procurement process works?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Introducing Procurement Processes learning journey.


Overview
This course is a beginner course designed to give consultants who are completely new to the procurement area an overview of how the basic procurement process generally works. These could, for example, be consultants who have previously worked in other areas and are now being used in the procurement process. They should then be able to distinguish the processes between SAP S/4 HANA and SAP Ariba and be able to identify where there is potential for integration between the two solutions.

Learning objectives
At the end of this course, the learner will be able to:

  • Demonstrate the fundamental procurement principles, practices, and processes, enabling them to navigate the procurement lifecycle effectively.
  • Develop the skills necessary to analyze procurement requirements, select appropriate procurement methods, and manage supplier relationships efficiently within diverse organizational contexts.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Check the related Live Sessions and engage and learn with SAP experts and peers.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 2 – Early Planning before Service Order Release
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management.

The concept of Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution is introduced in the following blog:

Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution

Within Service with Advanced Execution, there are various process flows depending on the business scenario. These process flows are distinguished based on two aspects:

  • How the process starts, and which is the first transaction.
  • What is the trigger for the creation of the maintenance order and eventually the start of planning.

Process Flow 2:

This blog showcases the process flow wherein the initial transaction is the service order, and the maintenance order is created before the release of the execution order item in the service order. This means the maintenance order is created and planning is started before the commercial aspects were finalized by the Service Manager.

This approach is especially beneficial when a lead time is required before starting the service execution. For example, if a spare part is required to start the service and it takes 10 days to order and procure it. The maintenance planner can proactively initiate the procurement process a couple of weeks before the scheduled start of the job. This way, the necessary components are available on time, avoiding delays and ensuring the service can be performed as per the planned schedule.

To enable early creation of a billable maintenance order, the following conditions must be met:

  • The execution order item must be in the In Process status.
  • The execution order item does not have any associated billable maintenance order.
  • There are no errors on the execution order item.

Creation of Service Order

A service order can be created using the ‘Create Service Order’ App.

The following details could be filled while creating a service order document:

  • Sold-to-Party
  • Organizational data such as Sales Organization and optionally Service Organization
  • Parties involved such as Service Employee Group and Employee Responsible
  • Execution Order Item with Planning Plant, Work Center, DIP Profile and Billing Relevance
  • Reference Objects

After entering all the details, save the service order document. The service order gets created with an execution order item with lifecycle status as ‘Open’. The lifecycle status of the execution order item can be changed to ‘In Process’ while the scope of the service and other details of the item are being negotiated between the Service Manager and the customer.

In the Items assignment block, filter the items to display only the execution order items and the Send to Planning column will be displayed. To create the billable maintenance order, the Send to Planning checkbox can be selected for the execution order item in ‘In Process’ status. The billable maintenance order is created in 'CRTD' (Created) status. The execution status on the execution order item is set as ‘Execution Planning’ as soon as the maintenance order is created.

The document flow between the service order and maintenance order can be viewed in Transaction History assignment block.

The details of the maintenance order connected to the execution order item can be viewed in the Maintenance Order assignment block in the item details page.

Status lifecycle in Service with Advanced Execution

Current state of an execution order item

The state diagram illustrates the status lifecycle of one execution order item along with its corresponding maintenance order in this process flow. Each block below represents a state displaying 3 different status values. The lifecycle and execution status of an execution order item along with the status of the corresponding maintenance order.

Note: The execution order item can be rejected only when the execution status is ‘Execution Planning’.

Release of Maintenance Order

Once the maintenance order is created from the service order, the maintenance order is in planning phase. After the planning is done and once the spare parts are available, the maintenance order is set to ‘REL’ (Released) by the Maintenance Planner. Subsequently, the execution order item is also set to ‘Released’ automatically and the execution of service can be started. At this stage, the execution status on the execution order item is set to ‘In Execution’.

Completion of Execution Order Item

After the execution of service is completed by the Maintenance Technician, the maintenance order status is set to ‘TECO’ (Technically Completed).  With this status transition, the lifecycle status of execution order item is set to ‘Completed’ automatically. The execution status on the execution order item is set to ‘Execution Done’.

Summary

The distinct feature in this process flow is the creation of maintenance order before the release of the execution order item in the service order. This helps the maintenance planner to start early planning to accommodate a lead time before the start of execution. As we proceed with the execution of the job, the lifecycle and execution status of execution order item are in continuous alignment with the status of maintenance order at every step.

 

Further references

To know more about the execution order item and its attributes, please refer following blog:

Deep Dive: Execution Order Item and Billable Maintenance Order

entry.title: 2024 SAP BTP Year in Review: Release Highlights for SAP S/4HANA users
entry.content:
2024 was an action-packed year in SAP Business Technology Platform solutions, with great technology updates to help you think ahead, adapt and keep things running smoothly.  This article highlights some of the new features released in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) solutions between January 2024 and November 2024 that are most relevant for SAP S/4HANA users.

As a quick recap, SAP Business Technology Platform brings together application development, automation, integration, data and analytics, and AI capabilities into one unified environment optimized for SAP applications. SAP BTP is designed to complement both SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition.  There are more than 80 different services in SAP BTP, in this article I'll focus on SAP Integration Suite, SAP Business AI, SAP Build, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP DataSphere, and SAP HANA Cloud.

So, what’s new? Let’s explore them now!

SAP Integration Suite: Supporting a wider range of integration needs

As a critical part of SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite has been a reliable source of integration technology for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. And the introduction of new features is making these connections more streamlined, situation aware, and productive—no matter how complex the landscape.

  • Event-driven architecture
    SAP Integration Suite now supports event-driven architecture to enable real-time processes that scale efficiently across distributed environments. It includes advanced event mesh capabilities that allow organizations to handle peak loads with greater ease and run more responsive and resilient operations.

  • Expanded integration library
    The curated library of integrations now includes ServiceNow and Workday solutions, expanding our portfolio to more than 3,000 connectors. Built and maintained by SAP and our partners, these integrations simplify connectivity to third-party applications—enabling smoother workflows and faster deployment.

  • On-premise solution workload management
    Extending beyond cloud-based connections, SAP Integration Suite supports ground-to-ground processes so companies can connect on-premise systems directly. This functionality provides greater flexibility for organizations with hybrid or fully on-premise environments.

  • API fabric for centralized access
    The API fabric enhances developer productivity by centralizing SAP and third-party APIs in a unified catalog. Developers can now quickly discover, register, and access a broad array of APIs, simplifying integration development and accelerating project timelines.

  • EOIO processing
    To support complex asynchronous integration scenarios, SAP Integration Suite now includes “exactly once in order” (EOIO) processing. This capability helps ensure messages are delivered exactly once and in the correct sequence while reducing data errors and enhancing consistency.

🔖Learn more about SAP Integration Suite's new features.

SAP Business AI: Making autonomous finance a reality

 The introduction of autonomous finance capabilities in SAP BTP is making a finance function that requires minimal human intervention a practical and productive reality. Enabled by the addition of SAP Business AI, this update automates complex financial operations—helping companies run with improved agility and resilience.

Leveraging the advanced AI and machine learning models of SAP Business AI, the platform provides SAP S/4HANA Cloud with tools that automate routine tasks, such as transaction processing and reconciliations. ​Recent benchmarking of SAP customers shows that adopting these capabilities paves the way to 90% more-efficient financial reporting, 7% higher forecast accuracy, and 10 times faster planning cycles.

In addition, the enhanced predictive capabilities and real-time data analytics available in SAP BTP equip SAP S/4HANA Cloud with insights that go beyond traditional finance automation. Organizations can now manage risk, increase transparency, and improve accuracy as the cloud ERP anticipates financial outcomes and adapts proactively to changes in cash flow or market volatility.

🔖Learn more about autonomous finance

SAP Build: Stepping up development with ABAP and generative AI

SAP Build solutions have become critical tools for the fast development of applications and automations with a simplified and streamlined builder interface.  Announced in October 2024, The latest integration of ABAP development tools and generative AI in SAP Build is making that experience even better for developers of all skill levels looking to modernize and extend their ERP capabilities.

By connecting tools such as ABAP, Java, JavaScript, and low-code in a unified environment, SAP Build enables direct development access within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making customization faster and easier. The extensibility wizard allows extensions for fields, logic, and processes while maintaining core system integrity.

Prebuilt content in SAP Build accelerates work across functions, including HR, spend management, and procurement. For example, the Joule copilot boosts efficiency with code completion, workflow automation, and instant help access. Developers can also create custom AI skills with drag-and-drop simplicity.

🔖Learn more the latest in SAP Build for SAP S/4HANA

SAP Datasphere: Making data handling simpler and more reliable

SAP Datasphere focused on improving access to mission-critical business data across any cloud infrastructure with a business data fabric.  In July 2024, the following updates gave business users stronger control over data views, resources, and import flexibility.

  • Compatibility contracts
    By setting a data view’s release state to “Released,” business users can help ensure output columns remain consistent until they’re replaced by a successor. This feature also enables gradual decommissioning while maintaining system integrity.

  • Workload management
    Resource management can be centralized by defining priorities and statement limits for all spaces. In return, business users can manage workload distribution effectively, while deprecating parameters for the command-line interface simplify updates in future releases.

  • Space changes during content import
    Specific spaces can now be chosen during imports. Space mapping added flexibility to support intra-tenant transport. Business users can import content into different spaces within the same tenant, even creating multiple copies in various spaces if needed.

🔖Learn more about SAP Datasphere's new features

SAP Analytics Cloud: Improving insight depth and speed

SAP Analytics Cloud, also part of SAP BTP, has been enhanced with several powerful updates that streamline data analysis, planning, and presentation. With these new features, SAP Analytics Cloud is adding even more depth and flexibility for customers using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making it easier for teams across the business to access insights and make data-driven decisions.

Here’s a look at some of the most impactful advancements:

  • Report-report interface
    Organizations with live connections to the SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW) application can now leverage the report-report interface to analyze data in context. With this feature, users can jump from making queries in SAP BW—from generating ABAP reports and transactions to analyzing data.

  • Natural language queries
    Enabled by generative AI, the just ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud allows users to ask questions in their natural language and receive instant data-driven answers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud while reducing their dependence on IT support. Recent updates include tools to simplify the user experience, such as the prompt guide, model control options for administrators, and advanced comparisons.

  • Plan-data files uploading
    Starting with the Q3 2024 release, business users are now able to enhance their planning efficiency by uploading their plan-data files from SAP S/4HANA Cloud directly into SAP Analytics Cloud. They can initiate uploads through triggers in stories, and administrators can set up data structures and upload jobs to help ensure data consistency.

  • Standard unit conversion
    The standard unit conversion feature simplifies data handling by allowing modelers and planners to use predefined conversion tables for measurements like length and weight. This capability helps ensure consistent unit representation across reports and stories, supporting data accuracy in SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

SAP Analytics Cloud (part 2): Extending planning and analysis business-wide

This year’s updates also brought powerful enhancements for planning and analysis in SAP Analytics Cloud. The following innovations were added to SAP Analytics Cloud to further streamline workflows, foster collaboration, and support strategic insights across departments, particulary for users with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  • Triggering of calendar events from external applications
    Enhancing workflow automation for enterprise planning scenarios, this capability simplifies end-to-end planning processes and helps ensure key tasks are executed without disruption across integrated platforms.

  • Automated synchronization of calendar events
    With automatic synchronization of SAP calendar events with Microsoft Teams and Outlook, users can keep track of critical planning deadlines and events. This integration improves productivity and collaboration, embedding SAP planning directly into users’ daily calendars.

  • Industry-specific business content
    New and updated content packages in SAP Analytics Cloud provide prebuilt templates and analytics tools for business areas, such as sustainability, finance, sales, and the public sector. These packages accelerate the implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, allowing companies to leverage industry-specific insights more quickly and effectively.

  • Probability simulation
    The compass functionality of SAP Analytics Cloud facilitates probability simulations that allow organizations to anticipate risks and model scenarios across key business drivers. Decision-makers can simulate the best, worst, and realistic outcomes—leading to more-proactive strategies to mitigate emerging disruptions.

  • Continuous planning integration
    The integration feature for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere forms a unified, governed data environment for planning and analytics. By centralizing data on one platform, this feature fosters collaboration across departments and reduces time-to-action for mission-critical decisions.

🔖Learn more about the new features in SAP Analytics Cloud

SAP HANA Cloud: Extending AI support and larger data volumes

 

SAP HANA Cloud is SAP’s database as a service solution, designed for large scale deployments.  The new features in SAP HANA Cloud for 2024 provide improved support for artificial intelligence and larger deployements, along with many quality of life features. Here are just a few of the highlights:

  • General availability of the Vector Engine for SAP HANA Cloud
    The introduction of the vector engine allows SAP HANA Cloud to provide the foundation for large language models, facilitating more contextually accurate responses to business queries. Learn more in this related blog post.

  • Support for larger instances
    With the 2024 Q2 release, the amount of memory available to allocate to a database instance increased to 12 TB. This allows better support for memory-intensive workloads.
  • Cloud Reliability – Backup Compression
    New backup compression for data, log, and catalog backups for SAP HANA database instances in SAP HANA Cloud reduces the amount of backup storage consumed, while ensuring the integrity and security of the backups.

  • Online migration
    The newly introduced online migration capabilities allow critical business processes to remain operational within the source SAP HANA system during the migration process. This innovation minimizes migration downtime, automates migration tasks, and reduces the need for manual interventions.

  • New machine learning options
    Multiple new features to the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) in SAP HANA Cloud facilitates faster modeling and application of AutoML, and automate analytics processes. The features allow a single neural network model to predict multiple related target columns. Other enhancements include the hierarchical density-based clustering of data with noise (HDBSCAN) and support for clustering of vector data using the DBSCAN and HDBSCAN functions.
    Learn more in the blog post provided by Christoph Morgen, as well as in this community post
  • Support for AWS PrivateLink services
    This new capability enables users to connect applications running within an Amazon Web Services (AWS) to SAP HANA Cloud using private IP endpoints, enhancing security. Learn more in this demo video.

Many more new features are described by @thomashammer in his blog post series.  

🔖 Learn more about SAP HANA Cloud

Closing

These new features in SAP BTP don't just make it easier to get the most from the data and processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud—they also build a solid foundation for growth and adaptability. When you use these tools together, you’re creating an ERP experience that’s ready for whatever comes next.

Learn more about how SAP BTP enhances SAP S/4HANA Cloud at sap.com/btp-for-s4hana.

 

Explore more related content in this series about SAP BTP with S/4HANA:

Data Migration

  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP

Extensions

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

Artificial Intelligence

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP

entry.title: Announcing Limited Preview of SAP LogServ integration with Microsoft Sentinel for SAP RISE customers
entry.content:
This blog was co-authored by Yossi Hasson (Sentinel Product Lead, Microsoft), Martin Pankraz (SAP PM, Microsoft), Aarthi Kannan (ECS Cloud Security Architect) & Krishna Rajapantula (ECS Lead of Security Engineering Operations)

We are thrilled to announce the limited preview of the integration between SAP LogServ and Microsoft Sentinel, exclusively for SAP RISE private cloud edition customers around the globe.

SAP LogServ is an SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) service aimed at collection, storage, forwarding and access of logs. LogServ centralizes the logs from all systems, applications, and ECS services used by a registered customer. 

Main Features include: 

  • Near Realtime Log Collection: With ability to integrate into customer SIEMs or Log Management systems. 
  • Log Retention: Retain logs indefinitely. Adjustable retention policy for each data source. With retention period of 365 days. 
  • Recovery of logs: Ability to recover logs which were retained. 

Microsoft Sentinel, a key component of Microsoft’s unified security operations platform, is setting new standards in cybersecurity innovation. It uses AI and automation to detect, investigate, and respond to threats swiftly. Microsoft Sentinel powers capabilities such as advanced threat hunting and security signal correlation enriched with SAP insights, offering a cutting-edge solution to protect critical business data. 

The innovative integration with SAP LogServ enables SAP RISE customers to leverage the powerful security analytics and threat intelligence capabilities of Microsoft Sentinel, seamlessly integrated with the log management features of SAP LogServ. This collaboration aims to enhance your security operations and provide comprehensive protection beyond your SAP environments to your whole IT estate. 

LogServ complements the existing SAP application layer threat monitoring and detections in Sentinel with the log types owned by SAP ECS as the system provider. This includes logs like: 

SAP Security Audit Log (AS ABAP), HANA database, AS JAVA, ICM, SAP Web Dispatcher, SAP Cloud Connector, OS, SAP Gateway, 3rd party Database, Network, DNS, Proxy, Firewall..... logs  

The list is growing as they become available, and SAP ECS is onboarding more. 

Benefits of the Integration 

  • SAP-centric Setup: Using SAP LogServ, and the agentless Microsoft Sentinel connection via SAP Business Technology Platform ensure a robust and SAP-cloud ready approach. 
  • Enhanced Security: Gain deeper insights into potential threats and vulnerabilities spanning beyond your SAP systems looking at your IT estate using the Microsoft Unified Security Operations Platform which combines XDR and SIEM into one holistic view. 
  • Centralized Monitoring: Streamline your security operations by adding the log data from SAP LogServ next to the SAP application layer logs into Microsoft Sentinel. 
  • Leading Automated Response: Using Microsoft Sentinel’s leading SOAR capabilities, LogServ may contribute to fully automated attack disruption or semi-automated SAP user blocking via Microsoft Teams. 
  • Proactive Threat Detection: Profit from out-of-the-box security content and utilize advanced analytics and machine learning in Microsoft Sentinel to identify and respond to threats in real-time. 

How to Sign-Up 

To participate in the limited preview of this integration, SAP RISE customers are invited to sign up by reaching out to their: 

  • ECS CDM or ECS TSM and 
  • Putting sap-logserv-sentinel-integration@service.microsoft.com on cc. 

Use the subject line: "Sign-Up for SAP LogServ and Microsoft Sentinel Integration Limited Preview." Please include your SAP RISE customer details in the email. 

SAP will check the eligibility of your RISE project and process your request accordingly. 

Get Started Today 

Don't miss this opportunity to enhance your security posture with the powerful combination of SAP LogServ and Microsoft Sentinel. Sign up today and be among the first to experience the benefits of this groundbreaking integration. 

We look forward to your participation and to helping you incorporate your SAP RISE environments into your overall IT estate. 

Big shoutout to Aarthi, Krishna, Martin and Yossi

entry.title: VCH AFL – Installation, Upgrade, Troubleshooting
entry.content:
General

The Application Function Library (VCH AFL) is fundamental to AVC (Advanced Variant Configuration). It’s written in C++ and is technically a plug-in running on the SAP HANA DB script server. The VCH AFL evaluates all the object dependencies in a configuration model with processing mode “Advanced.” This concerns all dependencies not attached to bill of material items (BOM items), also called “high-level dependencies.”

The VCH AVC lifecycle is managed by SAP in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, so the following information applies only to S/4HANA (on-premise) and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Here, customers have to manage installation and upgrades themselves.

 

Installation

The VCH AFL can be downloaded from the software center (https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter). Search for the VCH AFL in Software Downloads and add the S/4HANA version, e.g., “VCH AFL 2023”. After that, filter for the appropriate SAP HANA DB version (= SAP HANA revision), e.g., 79.01.

Please note that the patch level of the VCH AFL does not relate to the patch level of SAP HANA: VCH AFL with patch level 2 and SAP HANA patch level 0 work perfectly fine together. However, you should always take the highest patch level available. A general AFL note explaining the versioning can be found here: https://me.sap.com/notes/1898497/E. How to install the VCH AFL and which post-installation steps are necessary are described in the S/4HANA installation guide. You can find this guide on the main page of the help portal (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE). Scroll down to the section “Implement,” subsection “Guides”:

The Installation Guide points to the SAP HANA Server Installation and Update Guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/2c1988d620e04368aa4103bf26f17727/7eb0167eb35e4e2885415205b8383584.html?version=LATEST. The following note describes the manual installation of the VCH AFL: https://me.sap.com/notes/2987015 (S/4 Release 1909OP and following).

 

Update

When using the AVC, please always keep SAP HANA DB and VCH AFL up to date, as explained here: https://me.sap.com/notes/3318944. Bug fixes and performance improvements are usually only available with the latest revisions of the AFL. When you update the SAP HANA revision, first uninstall the old VCH AFL, then update SAP HANA, and then install the new VCH AFL. Check section no. 7 ("Updating the SAP HANA System") in the SAP HANA Server Installation and Update Guide for further additional components that might need a simultaneous update in your current setup.

When upgrading the SAP HANA DB, you can look for the latest VCH AFL patches in the software center. The SAP HANA DB installer does not always contain the latest VCH AFL.

 

Configuration

You can also manually configure some parameters of the VCH AFL concerning memory allocation or deletion of expired sessions. For details, refer to this note: https://me.sap.com/notes/2551305/E.

 

Dos and Don’ts and Troubleshooting

Run report VCH_HL_AFL_CHECK in transaction SA38 to check whether VCH AFL is installed correctly. If an error occurs, scroll down to see the error message. If the report results in errors but you are sure to have followed the installation guide, check whether one of the scenarios described below applies. If not, please create a ticket on component LO-VCH-ENG-AFL.

  • For SAP HANA revisions up to 59.14 or up to 79.01 (depending on the code line), the following issue exists: If you restart the index server of the DB while the script server is still running, the VCH AFL stops working. You can see how long the servers have been running in transaction DBACOCKPIT under Configuration Services. If the script server is older than the index server, restart it. See also https://me.sap.com/notes/3511162. 
  • In S/4HANA releases up to 2021, the temporary tables used for communicating between the VCH AFL and AS ABAP (ABAP application server) can accumulate, resulting in significant memory consumption. Please see https://me.sap.com/notes/3218076/E for how to fix this.
  • When running S/4 2022 FPS 0, please apply note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003247490 to get the VCH AFL to work (this is also mentioned in the release notes).

 

Further SAP Notes

  • “Upgrade VCH AFL of Advanced Variant Configuration for Faster Communication Between ABAP and VCH AFL”: https://me.sap.com/notes/3504987. This note is the prerequisite for https://me.sap.com/notes/3504072 (see below).
  • “Composite note: AVC Performance”: https://me.sap.com/notes/3280331. This composite note also contains the following note.
  • “Introduction of 'One Parameter Interface for AFL Communication'”: https://me.sap.com/notes/3504072

entry.title: #07 - Exploring Your SAP Solution: Engaging Stakeholders Early in the Implementation Process
entry.content:
Why is it important to familiarize yourself with your solution prior to implementation?

Let me share a story about a customer I worked with who took the time to enable their team before starting the project implementation and design discussions.

This customer was new to SAP and purchased a solution that was unfamiliar to them, yet critical for their business. As we recommend to all our customers, we advised them to undergo solution enablement before project implementation. This approach would help them become more familiar with the solution and maximize its value throughout the implementation process.

You’re probably wondering how it went for that customer. Well, I can tell you—it went really well!

When we began the design workshops, we presented the standard capabilities, as we always recommend to our customers for creating a scalable solution. However, we also recognize that business-specific requirements often call for adjustments. During these discussions, our customer was not only better prepared to engage with the topics and explanations we provided, but they were also able to identify key functionalities that would help enhance their business.

These design discussions are crucial during project implementation because they define the foundation of the customer’s solution. If the design is not done correctly from the start, the customer may end up with a solution that isn’t viable. Moreover, having the right solution design in place ensures that, during the testing phase, you're validating the solution effectively—rather than testing irrelevant processes that don’t align with the business needs. And that’s exactly what happened—my customer ended up with a solution that perfectly met their needs.

Another benefit was the time efficiency. By enabling themselves early on, the workshop discussions ran smoothly and stayed within the defined timelines, allowing us to progress without any delays. And it wasn’t just in the early stages where we were time-efficient; throughout all phases of the project implementation, we stayed on track because the scope of what needed to be delivered was clear from the start.

Finally, the entire team was engaged from day one and understood the impact of implementing a new solution. Typically, when a new solution is being implemented, not all users immediately embrace the change. Senior leadership is eager to move to a new system to improve business performance, increase margins and save costs, but business users are often resistant, especially when they are unfamiliar with the new system.

However, by enabling key stakeholders early on, we were able to make a significant impact on the skeptical business users. These stakeholders were able to champion the solution, sharing the added value it brought for the business users by streamlining their daily tasks. This empowered the business users to engage during the discussions and focus on higher-value activities, keeping them motivate. It also reinforced that everyone was contributing to the overall success of the business.

 

In a nutshell:

Gaining knowledge about SAP solution ahead of time is crucial, allows customers to maximize the value of the system from day one. 

By familiarizing themselves with the features, functionalities, and best practices before implementation, customers can ensure a smoother transition, reduce potential challenges, and align the solution more effectively with their business goals. This proactive approach allows teams to ask informed questions, make strategic decisions, and prepare their organization for a more seamless integration.

Furthermore, early exposure to the SAP solution empowers customers to take full advantage of its capabilities, ensuring they are ready to leverage its tools and insights to drive business success once the system goes live.

Stay curious and never stop learning!

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Ana Barreira serves as the Global Onboarding Advisor for the SAP Transportation Management (TM) solution within the Supply Chain Management area. She provides expert guidance and support to new SAP customers from contract signature through to successful go-live. With eleven years of experience—three of which have been as a Global Onboarding Advisor, and eight years as an SAP Consultant—Ana specializes in the SAP TM solution. She has successfully supported full-cycle customer implementation projects, assisted partners, and contributed to internal enablement efforts

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @Ana_Barreira  or @Linh-Hoang .

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: SAP Activate for RISE with SAP: Roadmap Updates & New Content!
entry.content:
My original Blog Post about SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition has gotten over 17,000 views - thank you all so much for your interest! But since then, the SAP Activate team has made some significant updates to the SAP Activate roadmap for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and a lot of new content has been released. 

In the following blog I want to highlight the following updates, that we have made since the publication of that last blog. We made more changes throughout the years and weeks since we publish on a bi-weekly release, but these are the most important and recent updates

  • Clean Core Strategy 
  • Selective Data Transition Path 
  • SAP Cloud ALM 
  • SAP Signavio 
  • SAP LeanIX
  • Release Notes 

Clean Core Strategy 

A key change that customers will notice is the introduction of the Clean Core Strategy, announced at the beginning of the year. The 'clean core' is a concept designed to achieve modern, flexible, and cloud-compliant ERPs. For an up-to-date ERP, customers achieve a clean core by integrating and extending the system in a cloud-compliant way, while governing master data and business processes. This approach ensures that when it comes time to upgrade a system, changes can be implemented without significant manual efforts to test and adapt existing structures. 

    Figure 1: Criteria for a Clean Core

SAP Activate offers advice for customers who want to keep their ERP systems clean from unnecessary changes. This way, their organization can take advantage of the latest release with minimal modifications and extensions & customizations that work well in the cloud. SAP Activate contains recommended tasks in the Clean Core Success Plan. It is available as a Tag that you can access on the Content tab. One of the main steps is to set up a governance for clean core by creating the Solution Standardization Board in Prepare phase and regularly performing governance activities, including capturing the action plan in dedicated runbook project in SAP Cloud ALM to track your progress towards clean core objectives. 

To get you up to speed on Clean Core, check out these resources: 

    • ERP Clean Core Strategy
    • SAP Activate Minute for RISE with SAP Clean Core
    • Clean Core related enhancements in the Solution Standardization Board for RISE with SAP.
    • Enhanced RISE with SAP Methodology with clean core quality checks
    • Learn here how to setup a dedicated runbook in SAP Cloud ALM

Selective Data Transition

Another key change that was made is that we added a new transition scenario in the SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The Selective Data Transition.

With the Selective Data Transition, you can migrate entire or selective historical data into SAP S/4HANA using high-speed and flexible data migration process. With a selective data transition, the source system is an SAP ERP system, while the target will be the SAP S/4HANA system, which can be an empty shell, a newly implemented, or an already productive system. The migration typically requires minimal downtime and can be executed either in a single step or in waves. It offers a balanced blend of the New Implementation and System Conversion approaches, combining their advantages to provide customers greater flexibility in data transfer and harmonization when transitioning to SAP S/4HANA. 

The content for this transition path was added via a tag in the roadmap viewer, where you can find deliverables, tasks and accelerators to help you understand the transition path and how SAP can support you. 

SAP Cloud ALM 

SAP Cloud ALM enhances the Activate methodology by delivering support for project management and execution. Over the years, we have introduced numerous SAP Activate templates within SAP Cloud ALM. When initiating a project, customers can select a specific implementation roadmap to guide their transformation journey.

For instance, the new selective data transition scenario mentioned above, is now supported by a dedicated template in Cloud ALM. This template is updated bi-weekly with the latest changes, which are accessible through the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer.

When starting a new project, customers can easily select the appropriate template to start their transformation journey. (Refer to Figure 2)

Figure 2: SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation 

Additionally, customers leveraging Cloud ALM and the Business Transformation Center can unlock even greater value. The Business Transformation tool, integral to the selective data transition process, is seamlessly integrated within Cloud ALM. 

In the image below, customers can see how Cloud ALM enables seamless navigation from the project implementation tab directly to the transformation project to use Business Transformation Center, during a Lean selective data transition scenario. 

Figure 3: SAP Business Transformation Center in Cloud ALM

Learn more by watching this great enablement Lean Selective Data Transition Overview Video. 

Additional transition scenario for SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition are also available in SAP Cloud ALM and updated bi-weekly. 

SAP Signavio

SAP Signavio helps accelerate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition transformation and business process performance. SAP Signavio is a platform that allows customers to model, analyze, optimize and execute business processes. SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub facilitates collaboration and communication on a project while SAP Signavio Process Manager helps customer re-imagine, document, model and simulate business processes. 

SAP Activate contains SAP Signavio procedure notes with details on how customers can leverage the tool during their transformation journey. You can also find specific Signavio solutions such as Process Insights, Process Intelligence, Process Governance are used in various tasks. It is available in the Product section that you can access on the Content tab.

To get you up to speed on SAP Signavio in SAP Activate, check out these resources: 

  • SAP Signavio Product Tag in SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Signavio Methodology for SAP S/4HANA Transformation

SAP LeanIX

SAP LeanIX is a solution that allows customers to manage and optimize enterprise architecture. SAP LeanIX provides a 360° overview of all applications, business capabilities, and IT components, enabling customers to gain control of their expanding IT landscape and create roadmaps for major transformation initiatives. Through collaborative and democratized data collection, out-of-the-box integrations, and fast insights through reports and diagrams, SAP LeanIX enables customers to manage enterprise architecture effectively. SAP LeanIX can be beneficial throughout an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Implementation, supporting initial assessment and creating a clear overview of the existing IT landscape. 

SAP Activate contains procedure notes on SAP LeanIX in relevant tasks, with more information on how customers can leverage the tool during their transformation journey. It is available in the Product section that you can access on the Content tab.

To get you up to speed on SAP LeanIX in SAP Activate, check out these resources: 

  • SAP LeanIX Product Tag in SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP LeanIX Home Page
  • Self-Enable on SAP LeanIX 

 

Release Notes 

To help you navigate these changes, you can refer to the release notes here. You'll find a summary of what's new, updated, or changed in the key deliverable tasks, and accelerators. The implementation roadmap is updated biweekly, so please be sure to check it regularly for the latest updates.  

Stay tuned for ongoing enhancements and future updates! We're always striving to improve our content, and your feedback plays a crucial role in that process. Feel free to share your thoughts and suggestions via the Roadmap Viewer page—we’d love to hear from you!

Head over to the release blog mentioned in the first section and explore the latest updates to SAP Activate for RISE with SAP.

Thanks again for reading

entry.title: Transform Your GenAI-Based BTP Experience: Digital Assistant with Data Learning & Adaptive Prompting
entry.content:
In this blog, we will discuss how to use BTP services to create a personalized digital assistant that will help you conduct your business objectives. The blog can help in improving the digital assistant's ability to learn from current data. By separating the AI prompt from the main application, we have also enabled flexible prompting. This allows us to easily change the prompt and prevents the need to repeatedly deploy the application.

Our Business Context: The customer, utilizing S4 HANA public cloud, has relied on an external application for end-user timesheet submissions. Given the critical role of timesheets in their revenue process, simplifying the submission method was a significant challenge. This led us to develop a GenAI-powered digital assistant for the customer. This assistant can analyze historical entries, provide timesheet suggestions, and confirm user responses, thereby streamlining the submission process. With the GenAI-based assistant, users will spend significantly less time on timesheet submissions.

Pre-Read: I'll make an effort to provide all of the references we used to create this digital assistant, along with information about how they relate to one another. Here are things to consider before we get into the specifics of each step.

  1. Before activating, please check your BTPEA/CPEA credits as there are few paid services you must subscribe to. The consumption cost of AI models is covered in the last section I added.
  2. Select the AI model that you wish to employ. Below is a detailed explanation of the steps.
  3. Currently, the SAP BTP GenAI service doesn’t support deep learning capacity. However, in this instance, we tried a different strategy and succeeded in allowing the AI to gain knowledge from the historical data. However, the learning will only take place during one session. That is, the learning will be lost when we reload the application.
  4. To put the solution into practice, a business consultant and a CAP developer are needed.

So, let’s get started.

The architecture of the genAI-enabled digital assistant is shown below. The SAP documentation page below has further information on this. https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-cap-genai-rag/blob/main/docs/architecture/multitenant-architecture.png

Fig 1: GenAI Architecture

Here, we developed an application for tracking and submitting timesheets. You may use business requirements to guide the design of your application. Let's go on to the steps.

  1. Subaccount setup. I won't go into specifics about how to set up a subaccount. The genAI services (AI Core, AI Launchpad) are not accessible in every region or from every provider. Please use SAP Discovery Center to check for availability (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/estimator/?commercialModel=btpea). If you have a subaccount set up currently and AI services aren't supported there, please make a new subaccount just for AI services. You can still work on the application in your original subaccount. Since AI services are not supported by our development subaccount, this blog discusses the dual-subaccount configuration. 

     

  2. Services to be Procured. These are the services that need to be purchased. To assist you in activating or configuring each service, I have included remarks next to it.

    Service / Subscription

    Price

    Remarks

    SAP AI Core - Generative AI hub

    Paid

    Select “Standard” or “Extended” option.

    SAP AI Launchpad

    Free

    Free Tier

    SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime

    Paid

    Available by default with CF

    SAP Cloud Management Service

    Free

    Activate if not there

    SAP Credential Store

    Paid

    Should be already there. If not, activate

    SAP HANA Cloud 

    Free

    Should be already there, if not activate the free one

    Each service may have a different price; however you can check the SAP Discovery Center for pricing information by using this link. https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/estimator/?commercialModel=btpea

     

  3. HANA DB setup
    1. For Context:  Each conversation's "context" is stored in HANA DB. We must also pass the historical conversation to genAI for every message received by the digital assistant. A single table contains all of this history.
    2. Flexible Prompting: In order to give the program flexible prompting, we also use a different table to contain the prompting text. The CAP application is related to this. By doing this, we can make sure that any changes to the prompt don't need redeploying the entire program.

       

  4. SAP AI Core Service: The most important phase in the development of this application is this one. The sub-steps to be taken are listed below.
    1. Activate the service “SAP AI Core”.
    2. Go to subaccount->Instances and Subscription-> Instances -> Open the Sap AI core service.
    3. Click on “Create Service Keys” to create new key for connection.
    4. Click on the generated key and pick the values of parameters “clientid” and “clientsecret”. Keep these settings handy since you'll need them when you use destination to link the application to AI Core.
    5. AI Launchpad setup – Configuration: Subscribe to the service “SAP AI Launchpad”.
    6. Select ML operations, Configurations and then Create configurationTraverse to subaccount->Instances and Subscription-> Subscriptions -> select “SAP AI Launchpad” and open the application.
    7. Select ML operations, Configurations and then Create configuration.
    8. You must enter a configuration name and fill in the properties as seen below in the create configuration screen (this is what I used, but you can modify it to suit your needs).
    9. In the next screen, give a model name and version. You can either chose default (which is what we did) or select other models. Currently supported models are gpt-35-turbo, gpt-4o, gpt-4, gpt-35-turbo-0125, gpt-35-turbo-16k, gpt-4-32k, gpt-4o-mini, text-embedding-ada-002, text-embedding-3-small, text-embedding-3-large.
    10. You don’t need to select any input parameters. Click on next, review and save. Now, the configuration is created.
    11. You are free to decide the model you wish to use.
    12. Deployment > take URL. 

       

  5. AI Launchpad setup – Deployment: Once the configuration is created, the next step is to create a deployment for the config. To do this, below are the steps:
    1. Open the created configuration (SAP AI Launchpad -> Configurations).
    2. Click on create deployment.
    3. Select the duration till when the deployment needs to be active, review and save.
    4. Scroll to “Deployments” section, select the currently created deployment and copy the URL until …ondemand.com. Note down this URL. 
    5. Destinations setup (to AI and to backend): After setting up all the configurations, we must build destinations to link the application to the backend and AI to the application. The subaccount where you plan to deploy your application (where Business Application Studio is activated) must have all these destinations created.
    6. Connect to AI Core and Launchpad.
      • Get the URL from the SAP launchpad deploy step (step 6.d of the blog) https://api.ai.prod.us-east-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com/
      • Authentication: “OAuth2ClientCredentials”.
      • Set client Id and client secret values from step 4.d.
      • Give the subaccount’s token service URL. This needs to be the token service URL of the subaccount which has the AI Core activated.
    7. To the backend: Create a destination like how you normally would do for any backend connection.

       

  6. Design directions for Source code development: Please be aware that this is not an extract of the entire source code. You may find instructions on how to use the code included in the SAP GitHub link here. https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-ai-core-simplifying-timesheet-tracking-task-in-s-4hana-cloud?files=1

    Prompt Design and Enabling Data Analysis

    In this situation, we must allow the AI Core to function as an assistant capable of carrying out the following tasks.

    1. Engage in productive user interaction.
    2. Examine data and produce insights that people can benefit from.
    3. Develop an API payload that can be used to upload data to the S4 HANA backend.

    This is how we can define the prompt and API calls to accomplish the aforementioned goals.

    • Define AI's role.
    • Transfer historical data to AI from S4, the S4 destination is used.
    • Direct API for data analysis, user feedback, and suggestion generation.
    • After the user confirms the input data, generate the payload.

    Below are the several types of texts and how the prompt is designed.

    Prompt Section

    Source

    Static prompt

    Separate HANA Db table 1- row 1

    Historic Data from SAP (backend)

    To be fetched from SAP

    First, you must retrieve the prompt pre-data from the database table. Next, you must use the second connection to retrieve the historic data from S4 and concatenate it all into a single string. In order for the AI to provide the payload in the proper format, you must also provide a sample output of the payload. Here is an example of a prompt.

    Please refer below links for best practices on prompt engineering.

    • OpenAI - https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/prompt-engineering
    • Google - https://developers.google.com/machine-learning/resources/prompt-eng

     

  7. Things to be considered during the build phase
    1. AI Core Connection within the application. Below are the steps to enable the AI Core in the Cap application and to make it available for usage.
      1. In Package.json, use CAP LLM plugin for connecting with AI ("cap-llm-plugin": "^1.3.1",
      2. Store environment variables in. cdsrc.json and connect to the AI destination created in step 7.a.
      3. Use the remaining part of deployment URL (post .com from step 6.d) in the CHAT_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_URL variable.
      4. Also add below environment variables
        1. Resource group
        2. API version.
        3. "CHAT_MODEL_RESOURCE_GROUP": "default",
        4. "CHAT_MODEL_API_VERSION": "2023-03-15-preview", Now we are all set and can access the AI Core from your Cap application
    2. Make calls to AI Core from CAP application (to get data, to post data into genAI, get data back from genAI). To call the AI core API, please follow below steps.
      1. Connect to “cap-llm-plugin”.
      2. Use methods from the llm plugin. We had used getChatCompletion method to post data into the AI core.
      3. The inputs provided by user needs to be concatenated into the chat history and to be stored in a HANA table.
      4. The chat history + new message will go into the llm plugin.
      5. The process will continue in a loop until the AI returns the final payload which needs to be used by the application to call the final S4 API to post data.
    3. Below diagram shows how the data is passed between the different components on program execution. With these steps and guidance mentioned below, you should be able to develop an AI enabled digital assistant which can process business transactions.:
      1. Accepts user input into the application.
      2. Store the user prompt/input into the chat history DB.
      3. Get the recent prompt + historical data from table.
      4. Pass this to the LLM Core.
      5. Get the output from LLM Core.
      6. Update the response into chat history DB.
      7. Display the LLM response to user.

         

  8. Additional Information

    Pricing

    While we need to choose the AI model to be used in our application, the cost of each model varies. Details on the costing details are available in the discovery center. https://ai-core-calculator.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/uimodule/index.html#/gen The estimate is calculated based on the AI tokens that we consume. You can assume that one token = ~4 characters.

    Use Case Identification

    While we had used to used AI to create a digital assistant, you can use the service for any other requirements you may have. Look at scenarios where there is natural language processing needed, summarizations needed, insights needed etc.

entry.title: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on RISE & SAP BTP

    🧰👷‍Toolbox for SAP Technology Architects👷‍🧰

 

 

RISE with SAP (S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition on SAP RISE) is re-writing the book on how companies "do" SAP, and infact, everybody on that journey or planning for or preparing for that journey is re-writing the book.

There are so many myths and misunderstandings about RISE, hopefully this work will shine more light on the subject.

The goal of this work is to provide a SAP RISE "1 Pager" for all of us Customer/System Integrator side folks who are on this journey. 

And back in 2013 when I was studying for SAP HANA Certification I published the following blog, as a central point of reference for SAP HANA technical information, it helped me with what I was studying and helped others with having piles of very useful information in one place:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/the-sap-hana-reference-for-sap-basis-administrators/ba-p/13007539

Fast forward 12 years, and we are now in the same situation with SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP, there is useful documentation all over the place and it would help if there was a central location for SAP Technology Architects to use as a "Toolbox". It will certainly help me 🙂

Implementing and Migrating to and Running S/4 PCE on RISE for the last two years, and as usual collecting a mountain of the most useful documentation as a personal toolbox, it's been clear to see that similar to the situation back in the earlier period of HANA, there is useful RISE documentation in a number of locations, this blog will act as a central resource pointing to all of the locations of the SAP S/4 PCE RISE documentation.

And similarly for the BTP, having been doing BTP (or as it was the SAP Cloud Platform) for 7 years, this blog will be a central location pointing to the most useful BTP documentation for SAP Technology Architects.

The two subjects are combined, SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE with BTP, because in my eyes the two go hand in hand, S/4HANA PCE on RISE is the next generation of the previous On-Premise ERP offerings from SAP, and the BTP contains the next generation of Applications which we will all use with the S/4HANA PCE on RISE, including for example, and not limited to, Integration Suite and CPI & EM/AEM & APIM, obviously CALM as this is replacing in many but not all cases what we do with SolMan, and all of the other Services which we will need pretty much day by day to run S/4HANA PCE on RISE, and of course, Steampunk, keeping the Core Clean, and "Upgrade Stable" (as my friend Sakthivel Sivaguru would say), then we need the ABAP Service in the BTP for the side car extensions. But that's just the BTP "Starter Pack", as you go on the BTP journey you will be using all kinds of Services and including the AI Joule and the Business Application Studio and the Work Zone, Custom Domain, ADS, Secure Logon, CTMS  etc and then of course the Intelligent Services across the Business Processes, eg Intelligent Account Reconciliation, there's a link to an Excel sheet here, which lists all of the Business Processes and how they are Intelligently enabled by SAP Technologies.

If you have links to useful documentation which you think will help others and should be added to the list, then please just write it in the comments and I will add it to the list.

For 25 years I have been hunting and collecting SAP documentation, my library spans the whole journey from before the New Dimension Products, through NetWeaver 2004, through HANA DB, through S/4HANA, through SAP Cloud Platform and on through the current phase S/4HANA Cloud aka "Grow with SAP", S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition aka "RISE with SAP", and the Business Technology Platform and the Intelligent Technologies. Hunting a collecting documentation is a useful skill, and here's a tip from my friend Jarod Sandham - some useful search criteria to find documentation is to search "https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/ <insert search criteria here>, eg open google and search for:  https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/  security

Please expect to find changes and new information here, because it will be a live work in progress, it's going to take a few weeks to list and classify everything that I already have. Plans for immediate future are for each link to add a screenshot of the most useful information behind that link, and that's going to take a few weeks, and at the same time the list will just get longer.

And finally the ask to SAP... if friends from SAP are reading this work, and know about new versions of documents/materials or new documents/materials which are available either publicly or with an S User and could enrich this work, please let me know either directly or in the chat and I will incorporate that material to the benefit of everyone.

Disclaimer: For the record, regarding the pictures and screenshots, ALL pictures are from documents which are publicly available and searchable on google, there are no pictures from any SAP documentation which is protected behind an authentication mechanism. There are many links here to documents where you will need to have an S User to be able to read them, and there are no screenshots from any of those documents. Every screenshot/picture contains a statement that this document is publicly available on the Internet.

 

 

Ok, let's go 🚀

 

 

Contents:

Section 1] General Introduction Documents and RISE Getting Started Guides

Section 1.01] The SAP & Customer Shared Responsibilities on RISE

Section 1.02] The S/4HANA 3 Transition & Migration & Implementation Options

Section 1.1] Implementing and Running S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on SAP RISE

Section 2] SAP ECS Enterprise Cloud Services

Section 2.1] Azure Useful Links

Section 2.2] AWS Amazon Web Services Useful Links

Section 3] Security on SAP RISE and ECS 

Section 4] Data Migration and Transition

Section 5] SAP BTP General

Section 5.1] SAP CALM Cloud Application LifeCycle Management

Section 5.1.1] Monitoring with SAP CALM - UNDER PREPARATION

Section 5.2] Extending S/4HANA on RISE - UNDER PREPARATION

Section 5.3] Integrating S/4HANA on RISE - UNDER PREPARATION

Section 6] SAP Customer Center of Excellence

Section 7] Lessons Learned

_________________________________________________________________

 

Section 1] General Introduction Documents and RISE Getting Started Guides

What is RISE from the viewpoint of a SAP Technology Architect ?  

The following picture is from the publicly available document Presentation Title

How do you do RISE ?

Regardless of what anybody thinks about all of the different methodologies, every SAP Customer who is contemplating the move to S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition on RISE must begin by reading about the "RISE with SAP Methodology".

This is basically a guided procedure including supporting documentation to help SAP Customers get to S/4HANA in the smoothest and straightest line possible. 

Naturally SAP have a recommended tool chain to support for following the SAP RISE Methodology, and regardless of whether on not you use SAP's recommended tools, this picture is very useful because it elaborates a little more what is happening at each part of the journey:

Here's an introduction to the methodology RISE with SAP | Methodology

This is the overview of the guided procedure:

This picture comes from this document which is publicly available: Mapping your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition : A practical guide for senior IT leadership

These are the core tasks of the methodology:

I recommend everybody who will be part of the journey to read that document as one of the first steps, once everybody has read that document, everybody will be on the same page as to what lies ahead.

Here are more documents which are very useful during the preparation:

SAP Activate Methodology for SAP S/4HANA Transformation

https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/AAE80671-5087-430B-9AA7-8FBE881CF548/phaseAccelerator/S4HANATRANSONPRE

https://me.sap.com/readinesscheck/home

RISE OnBoarding Resource Center

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise/rise-private.html

RISE OnBoarding Handbook Page

The Handbook is another MUST READ and is publicly available and contains a huge amount of useful practical information, like this, what happens after signing the SAP RISE Contract:

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise.html

 

Then you are reminded of the overview of who does what...

 

 

and then you are reminded that to be a Future Ready Enterprise you are going to need a BTP...

 

 

And there is a link to an excellent publicly available document on the Future Ready Enterprise with the BTP FRE Best Practice Guide for RISE Transformation_Private Cloud.pdf

BTP is covered in detail in the BTP section of the Toolbox

 

This is a really useful document, it walks you through a theoretical S/4HANA Transformation and including all of the steps Transformation Plan 1.0 - Outcome summary of SAP S/4HANA Adoption Starter

Moving to RISE Differentiating your Core ERP with S/4HANA PCE
